creator: alexander@imp.lg.ua
text:
When I work with samba-3.0.24d - all was fine, but when update to 3.0.25 the problem has appeared.
Some explanation:

I have samba share /home/alexander/my_share

and mounted .iso file, using mdconfig and mount_cd9660

/dev/md0 on /home/alexander/my_share/some-iso (cd9660, local, read-only)

when i trying to read some files from this folder with smbclient i got

"Receiving SMB: Server stopped responding
Call returned zero bytes (EOF) listing \CD-Server\*
Error in dskattr: Call returned zero bytes (EOF)"

and in /var/log/message

May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/fault.c:fault_report(41)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   ===============================================================
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/fault.c:fault_report(42)
May 30 16:28:16 imp kernel: pid 78768 (smbd), uid 0: exited on signal 6
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   INTERNAL ERROR: Signal 6 in pid 78768 (3.0.25)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   Please read the Trouble-Shooting section of the Samba3-HOWTO
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/fault.c:fault_report(44)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   From: http://www.samba.org/samba/docs/Samba3-HOWTO.pdf
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/fault.c:fault_report(45)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   ===============================================================
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/util.c:smb_panic(1632)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   PANIC (pid 78768): internal error
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/util.c:log_stack_trace(1786)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   unable to produce a stack trace on this platform
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]: [2007/05/30 16:28:16, 0] lib/fault.c:dump_core(181)
May 30 16:28:16 imp smbd[78768]:   dumping core in /var/log/samba/cores/smbd

Fix: 

rollback to 3.0.24d
How-To-Repeat: create md device and mount it into samba-shared dir
